# Questions in Livestream 47

Live Stream #47: Passover 2022.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=293" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:53</a> | What is the numerical meaning of 666? | He surveys major views, noting gematria is common, but also entertains an Old Testament allusion to Solomon's wealth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=543" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:03</a> | Is there linguistic reason to treat some monotheistic-sounding passages as ANE hyperbole? | He says Isaiah's language is better read as genuine monotheism, not mere hyperbole masking polytheism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:20</a> | What position do you take on henotheism, monotheism, and monolatry in the Bible? | He argues the biblical materials lean toward monolatry grounded in a broader monotheistic outlook. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=1160" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:20</a> | How could God take Enoch if Enoch is understood as a clan or progenitor figure? | He suggests Enoch may be the prominent end figure of a clan line rather than only its biological founder. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=1285" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:25</a> | In Genesis 47:7-9, was Jacob really 130 or 131? | Scholars debate it; he leans toward a missing ancestor or clan-age explanation, while noting an honorary or exaggerated age is also possible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=1599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:39</a> | Is man body and soul, or body, soul, and spirit? | He says the issue is debated, but he leans somewhat toward a tripartite model. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=1982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:02</a> | Could Ezekiel's Magog battle fit events around 1071-1097 under a preterist reading? | He says the parallels do not convince him and that the proposal does not really solve the passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=2467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:07</a> | Was Deuteronomy a Josianic forgery according to the documentary hypothesis? | He is unconvinced, arguing the hypothesis lacks internal consistency in how its advocates divide the Pentateuch. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=2740" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:40</a> | When was the Last Supper relative to Passover, given John's chronology? | He works through the preparation-day question and treats the timing problem as real but explainable within the Gospel data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=3022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:22</a> | Does Satan have any connection with Egyptian myth? | He says the biblical term and role develop in their own context and should not be simply collapsed into Egyptian mythic figures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=3456" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:36</a> | Do you think Judas Iscariot was a zealot? | The name Iscariot is suggestive and the idea is reasonable, but he has not studied it closely enough to be certain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=3734" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:14</a> | What part of the Holy Scriptures caused you more personal conflict? | Hebrews troubles him most because of its warnings about apostasy and people thinking they are in the faith when they are not. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=4112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:32</a> | Besides Pharaoh's dynasty, were there long-standing important noble families or dynasties in Egypt? | Yes; provincial noble families and nomarch lines could last for centuries and formed major power bases. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=4285" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:25</a> | If the Mount Ebal curse amulet is genuine, does it challenge the late Exodus date? | No; he says the archaeological context fits a later setting, and early-date claims rely too heavily on subjective paleography. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=5358" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:18</a> | How should we assess the claim that a king of Sumer was Kushite because Kush in Egyptian and Sumerian are the same without vowels? | He rejects it as a faulty etymology; cuneiform signs can have multiple phonemes, and the comparison does not work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=5592" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:12</a> | How was Messiah in the tomb for three days and nights from Friday to Sunday? | Jewish inclusive reckoning counts partial days; Friday before sunset, Sabbath, and Sunday morning can be counted as into the third day. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=5950" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:10</a> | Are Genesis 1 and 2 evidence of J and P plus a sloppy redactor, and is their similarity to ANE texts usually held by the same scholars? | It depends on the scholar; Genesis interacts with ANE material but is not simply copying it, and the documentary hypothesis does not solve the issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=6335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:35</a> | Do you have recommendations for resources on Gnosticism? | He cannot strongly recommend a general book; he mentions his own work but says many available resources have serious problems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=6596" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:56</a> | Are there suggested books or places to learn Hebrew wordplay outside a university or seminary? | He knows no single collected resource and usually looks to commentaries; biblical names often carry wordplay. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=6818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:38</a> | What are your thoughts about how widespread using the Lord's name in vain has become? | The main issue is bearing God's name lightly by misrepresenting him; expletive use is only a smaller part of the commandment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=7380" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:00</a> | What did you mean about having seen things that make atheism unlikely for you? | He says paranormal or demonic encounters make becoming an atheist very difficult for him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=7566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:06</a> | Is it fair to criticize there being no evidence for Israelites in Egypt if "Israel" starts only with Saul? | He says no; Israelite identity predates the monarchy, so that objection is not persuasive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=7761" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:21</a> | Could you explain Jonah's Sheol language and its relation to Christ? | He treats Jonah 2 as a strong typological text that helps explain Christ's use of Jonah imagery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=8260" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:40</a> | Is Goliath's height of six cubits and a span meant literally? | He sees no major problem with treating the giant stature as literal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=8456" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:56</a> | Any updates regarding the Mount Ebal amulet? | No decisive updates; scans and RTI images are not published, latest images are not compelling, and only a couple of letters seem clear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=8719" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:19</a> | Would Manfred Bietak agree that Israelites were at Avaris until the Ramesside dynasty? | He says Bietak's view shifted over time and that today he would likely accept at least a Ramesside or 19th-dynasty possibility. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=9439" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:19</a> | Is Easter derived from Ashtar, Ishtar, or Isis? | No; he treats that as false etymology and says the Christian date is tied to Passover and lunar calculations, not those deities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=9693" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:33</a> | What are your thoughts on James Kugel's *How to Read the Bible*, and is it worth it? | He has heard some good things but has not read it, so he will not recommend it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=9725" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:05</a> | What are your thoughts on Dr. Titus Kennedy? | He does not recommend him and says Kennedy plays fast and loose with evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=9856" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:16</a> | What are the identities of the nations separated at the Tower of Babel? | Unknown; it is too early before written history to identify the nations securely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=9969" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:09</a> | Is it irrational for lay people to disregard miracles a priori? | Yes, but he also warns against multiplying miracles where the Bible does not claim them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=10273" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:13</a> | How would someone construct a good case for young-earth creationism? | It would need to be made on scientific grounds while abandoning weak biblical backdating and hermeneutics. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=10550" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:50</a> | How misguided is the notion that Egyptians wrote everything down and we have their complete chronological history? | Very misguided; Egyptian records are fragmentary, mostly religious, and many administrative records are lost or poorly preserved. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=11024" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:44</a> | Were you educated under Alan Millard at Liverpool, and who were your most influential scholars? | No; Millard had retired. His supervisors were Mark Collier and Steven Snape, and Kenneth Kitchen was the strongest influence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KvObTy1LgnE?t=11474" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:14</a> | What is the historical timeline developed by a computer program that you liked, and where can it be found? | It is his own Groundhog Chronology program, available at `www.groundhogchronology.com`. |
